# 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka

*Generated by gpt-5 from 297 English News Articles published between 2026-06-08 and 2026-06-15.*

## 1. Sri Lanka’s most sensitive security case moved from rhetoric to legal process under the PTA (Prevention of Terrorism Act).

The arrest of the former state intelligence head triggered <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">an inquiry by the national rights commission</span> and drew interventions from the legal profession, while opposition figures and former lawmakers pressed for due process safeguards. Letters, court filings and monitoring visits were recorded, and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">a travel ban in a related bombing probe</span> added pressure to maintain procedural integrity.

Debate widened beyond one detainee to the system itself, with calls to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">fill higher-court vacancies</span>, to curb prolonged detention, and to align practice with treaty obligations that underpin trade preferences. A UN torture‑prevention mission scheduled for mid‑June added external scrutiny to the island’s rule‑of‑law credentials.

## 2. Cabinet ordered the drafting of a Digital Economy Bill to anchor policy, regulation and AI (artificial intelligence) support.

A concept note cleared earlier will now be converted into <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">primary legislation by the Legal Draftsman</span>, with a mandate to create <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">an empowered institution</span> for policy, implementation and enforcement. The framework is to attract skilled talent, formalise oversight and set out governance for emerging technologies.

Ministers said the design envisages <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">public‑private participation</span> and a single point for national digital policy execution. The brief also covers <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">innovation sandboxes</span>, interoperability for public platforms and clearer lines between regulator, operator and market.

## 3. The presidency convened emergency planning for an El Niño (Pacific warming pattern) season likely to mirror 2016–17.

Reservoir status, irrigation releases and hydropower scenarios were tabled, with agencies told to file <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">time‑bound contingency plans</span> for food supply, drinking water and electricity. Officials said the current cultivation had been <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">advanced by roughly six weeks</span> to hedge rainfall risk.

Water‑sharing rules, demand curbs and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">backup generation options</span> are to be pre‑authorised, while agriculture support will pivot to input timing and crop choices. The disaster agency will align <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">early‑warning</span> with reservoir operations to avoid last‑minute spill or shortage decisions.

## 4. Colombo Port City gained momentum as 77 strategic businesses were approved under the SEZ (special economic zone) law.

The list included three primary real‑estate anchors and dozens of services firms expected to bring foreign inflows over five years and to create several thousand direct roles. Tax and regulatory amendments passed earlier this year now allow <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">single‑window licensing</span> and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">fast‑track visas</span> tied to investment thresholds.

Regulators said secondary approvals target <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">traded services</span> such as logistics, finance, HQ operations and IT, with land leases to be executed directly by the commission. The master developer cited roughly <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">$1.4 billion</span> already spent on reclamation and enabling works.

## 5. A former deputy minister received a 16‑year prison term in a corruption case tied to vehicle rentals at a state lottery board.

The High Court said conduct that confers <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">undue advantage</span> and causes loss to the treasury <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">damages confidence in public institutions</span> and contributes to fiscal distress. The sentence comprised <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">four concurrent four‑year terms</span> ordered to run cumulatively, plus fines.

## 6. Authorities widened probes into bogus import remittances after lawmakers flagged an alleged Rs. 13 billion bank fraud.

Investigators reported thousands of <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">telegraphic transfers</span> routed through shell firms to dozens of countries <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">without matching customs entries</span>, citing one network that sent over <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">$42.7 million</span> and another near $43 million. A separate review found more than <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">26,000 outward payments</span> across 227 accounts over two years, with many entities dissolved shortly after use.

## 7. A new foreign‑exchange conversion rule hit equities as the rupee softened and bond yields steadied.

Shortening exporters’ dollar conversion windows weighed on risk appetite, pushing the main index lower on thin turnover while the currency was quoted around <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 335–342 per dollar</span> on official boards. Dealers reported lacklustre secondary‑market flows and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">a firm short end of the yield curve</span>.

## 8. A UN torture‑prevention delegation scheduled a second visit to assess detention safeguards and the national mechanism under OPCAT (Optional Protocol to the Convention against Torture).

Members will meet ministries, the national commission, custodial agencies and civil society, and conduct <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">unannounced site inspections</span>. A confidential report will follow, with publication contingent on <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">state consent</span>.

## 9. Sri Lanka faced a dengue surge as inspections found thousands of larval sites and legal action ramped up.

A three‑day campaign logged nearly 98,000 premises checked, <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">over 6,000 with larvae</span> and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">about a quarter flagged as high‑risk</span>, with factories and construction leading the ratios. Health data showed <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">close to 39,000 cases</span> this year, roughly half from the Western Province, prompting <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">more than 3,000 red notices</span> and over 2,400 prosecutions.

## 10. A union at the flag carrier opposed the new chairman’s appointment, citing unresolved complaints and deepening losses.

Representatives warned that leadership credibility is critical as restructuring advances, pointing to an <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">accumulated deficit above Rs. 600 billion</span>, fresh operating losses and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">a year‑long CEO vacancy</span>. A state‑led committee remains in place to steer the turnaround while the board transition proceeds.
